note:
7月1日，在任职七年多后即将离开Facebook的波士顿软件工程师马克斯·王(Max Wang)在该公司的内部讨论板上分享了一段旨在起到警告作用的视频。
“我认为Facebook正在大规模伤害人们，”他在视频附带的便条中写道。“如果你也这么想，也许可以给这块表一块。”
大多数员工在离开“马克·扎克伯格的作品”时，通常会发布他们公司徽章的照片，并附上道谢同事的告别信。王选择了一段自己直接对着镜头说话的片段。随之而来的是对Facebook在前一年的领导力和决策能力进行了24分钟的清醒抨击。
这段视频是几个月来内部冲突、抗议和离职的升华，此前该公司决定原封不动地保留唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统的一篇帖子，该帖子似乎呼吁对抗议警察杀害乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)的人采取暴力行动。虽然王的信息不一定是独一无二的，但他对公司持续未能保护用户的评估-这一评估得益于他在公司的漫长任期-提供了迄今为止对Facebook最令人震惊的尖锐指责之一。
“我们正在失败，”他说，批评Facebook的领导人以牺牲现实世界的伤害为代价来迎合政治关切。“更糟糕的是，我们已经将这一失败写入了我们的政策。”
Facebook在其各种社交平台上拥有约30亿用户，尽管自2016年总统大选前夕以来，外部对Facebook的批评一直存在，但很少在这家社交媒体巨头内部引发广泛的异议。在过去三年半的时间里，Facebook经历了一个又一个丑闻--俄罗斯干预选举、剑桥分析(Cambridge Analytica)、缅甸罗辛亚人种族灭绝--股价上涨，并继续招聘和留住顶尖人才。尽管偶尔会出现内部骚乱，但员工们普遍认为公司利大于弊。至少，他们避免了公开表达自己的不满。
Facebook工程师丹·阿布拉莫夫(Dan Abramov)6月26日在公司内部沟通平台Workplace上发帖称：“这一次，我们的反应感觉不同。”“我花了一些(带薪假期)来重新聚焦，但我无法动摇公司领导层辜负了我和同事们对他们的信任的感觉。”
根据对现任和前任员工的采访，以及BuzzFeed新闻获得的数十份文件，像王健林和阿布拉莫夫这样的信息表明，Facebook对总统经常引发分裂的帖子的处理，已经导致其队伍发生了翻天覆地的变化，并导致了人们对领导层的信任危机。这些文件-包括公司讨论线索、员工调查结果和扎克伯格的录音-显示，该公司在删除自己员工报告的含有白人民族主义和纳粹内容的广告方面行动迟缓。它们表明，该公司关于支持种族正义事业的公开声明与禁止Facebook用户利用公司资源支持政治事务的政策是如何背道而驰的。它们显示扎克伯格被公开指控误导员工。最重要的是，他们描绘了一种支离破碎的公司文化。
沮丧和愤怒的员工们现在正在全公司的会议上挑战扎克伯格和领导力，举行虚拟罢工，并质疑他们的工作是否让世界变得更美好。这场骚乱已经到了Facebook首席执行官最近威胁要解雇那些“欺负”同事的员工的地步。
在即将迎来美国总统大选之际，这家社交网络的一举一动都将受到剖析和分析，它面临着前所未有的内部异议，员工们担心该公司有意无意地对与特朗普相关的内容决策施加政治影响，并担心Facebook正在破坏民主。
Facebook前选举广告诚信主管雅尔·艾森斯塔特(Yyaël Eisenstat)表示，员工们的担忧反映了她在该公司的经历，她认为该公司正走在一条即将进入选举的危险道路上。
她在接受BuzzFeed新闻采访时表示：“所有这些措施都会导致这样一种情况：到了11月，部分Facebook用户将不会相信选举结果，因为他们在Facebook上收到了大量准备不信任选举结果的信息。”
她说，由乔尔·卡普兰(Joel Kaplan)领导的公司在华盛顿特区的政策团队试图不适当地影响她的团队做出的决定，公司最近未能对特朗普总统的帖子采取适当行动，这表明员工感到不安和担忧是正确的。
“这些都是非常明显的例子，不仅让我感到不安，也让Facebook的员工感到不安，让整个民权社区感到不安，也让Facebook的广告商感到不安。”如果你仍然拒绝倾听所有这些声音，那么你就是在证明你的决策受到了其他声音的指导，“她说。
你在Facebook或其他科技公司工作吗？我们很高兴收到你的来信。联系ryan.mac@buzzfeed.com或通过我们的举报热线渠道之一。
在一份广泛的声明中，Facebook的一位发言人在回应本文的一系列问题时表示，公司有严格的政策流程，并对员工如何做出决定是透明的。
“Facebook的内容决策是基于我们对书面公共政策的最好、最均匀的应用。一群人，甚至是员工，总是认为这些决定是不一致的；这就是广泛应用政策的本质，“这位发言人说。“这就是为什么我们实施了一个严格的流程，即在采用新政策时咨询外部专家，同时征求员工的反馈，也是为什么我们创建了一个独立的监督委员会，对Facebook上的内容政策决定提出上诉。”
在他的笔记中，在社交网络工作了四年的阿布拉莫夫将Facebook比作一座核电站。他说，与传统媒体不同，Facebook能够以前所未有的规模产生“社会能量”。
他写道：“但即使是小细节出错也可能导致灾难性的后果。”“社交媒体有足够的力量破坏我们的社会结构。如果你认为这是夸大其词，那你就没有注意到这一点。“。
5月28日，随着反对警察暴行的抗议活动在明尼阿波利斯和全国各地肆虐，唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统在他的Facebook和Twitter账户上发布了相同的信息，这两个账户总共有1.14亿粉丝。
“刚刚和州长蒂姆·沃尔兹通了电话，告诉他军方一直支持他，”总统当晚写道。“任何困难，我们都会承担控制权，但是，当抢劫开始时，枪击就开始了。”
在几个小时内，推特就将特朗普的帖子贴上了警告标签，指出这违反了美化暴力的规定。与此同时，Facebook什么也没做。它已经决定，“当抢劫开始，枪击就开始”这句话--这句话在历史上与种族压迫的警察暴力有联系--并不构成对其服务条款的违反。
第二天，扎克伯格在解释这一决定时说，虽然他对这篇帖子有“发自内心的负面反应”，但Facebook的政策允许“围绕国家使用武力进行讨论”。此外，他认为，尽管这句话有其历史背景，但它有可能被解读为总统只是在警告说，抢劫可能会导致暴力。(Axios后来报道称，扎克伯格在帖子发布后的第二天亲自给特朗普打了电话。)。
员工们已经对公司未能对特朗普在5月早些时候发布的一篇包含邮寄投票错误信息的帖子采取行动感到愤怒，他们进行了反抗。在一个名为“让我们修复Facebook(公司)”的工作场所小组中，一名员工开始了一项民意调查，询问同事们是否同意“我们领导层本周关于投票错误信息和可能被视为煽动暴力的帖子的决定”。该小组有大约1万名成员。约1000名受访者表示，该公司在这两个帖子上都做出了错误的决定，是排名第三的回答“我不确定”的回复数量的20多倍。
“我不知道该怎么做，但我知道什么都不做是不可接受的，”Facebook的Facebook设计经理杰森·斯蒂尔曼(Jason Stirman)那个周末在Twitter上写道，这是众多反对声音中的一个。“我是联邦调查局的一名员工，我完全不同意马克对特朗普最近的帖子采取任何行动的决定，这些帖子显然是煽动暴力的。”在FB里，我并不孤单。在种族主义问题上没有中立的立场。“。
接下来的周一，数百名员工-由于公司的冠状病毒政策，大多数是远程工作-将他们的工作场所头像换成了黑白相间的拳头，并在数字罢工中高喊生病。
随着Facebook努力应对另一场公关危机，员工士气暴跌。根据BuzzFeed新闻获得的数据，员工满意度指标--通过每周对数百名员工进行的“微脉”调查衡量--在特朗普抢劫职位的裁决后大幅下降。
6月1日，也就是罢工当天，约45%的员工表示，他们同意Facebook正在让世界变得更美好的说法--比前一周下降了约25个百分点。同一天，Facebook的内部调查显示，大约44%的员工对“Facebook领导力带领公司朝着正确的方向前进”充满信心--比5月25日下降了30个百分点。根据BuzzFeed News看到的数据，截至本月早些时候，对这个问题的回答一直停留在这个较低的水平附近。
BuzzFeed News重建的两张图表显示了Facebook MicroPulse调查衡量的员工满意度指标。
在文化上，Facebook已经变得越来越分裂，一些公司的忠诚者推动这样的想法，即沉默的大多数支持特朗普帖子上的电话。
在Blind上，一个要求人们匿名讨论雇主的工作电子邮件的论坛应用程序上，一些未透露姓名的员工要求解雇那些参与罢工的人。有一条帖子特别关注Facebook的产品管理总监杰森·托夫(Jason Toff)，在特朗普做出决定后，他曾在Twitter上发帖称，他“对我们的表现并不自豪”。
一位盲人用户在一篇名为“Fire Jason Toff”的帖子上写道：“他的主要职责是获取顶尖人才，激励他的团队，而当你公开分享你对自己工作的地方并不自豪时，这是不可能做到的。”
其他匿名的Facebook员工中伤了梅·周，这位软件工程师曾在一次全体员工会议上问道，有多少黑人员工参与了特朗普发布的这一决定。他们称她无礼，称她为“sjw”--“社会正义勇士”的贬义简称。(在回答周的问题时，扎克伯格表示，只征询了公司奥斯汀办公室的一名黑人员工的意见，Facebook的政治言论政策需要调整。)。
托夫拒绝为这篇报道发表言论。周没有回复记者的置评请求。
这种对Facebook文化的持续争斗和侵蚀激怒了扎克伯格。在6月11日与员工进行的现场问答中，他尖锐地谈到了这个问题。
“我一直很担心…。作为这些辩论的一部分，我们内部的许多人相互指责的不尊重程度，在某些情况下，还包括尖酸刻薄。“。“如果你再胁迫你的同事在某件事上表明立场，我们就会解雇你。”
2019年10月23日，在位于加利福尼亚州门洛帕克的Facebook公司总部园区，Facebook员工走过一块牌子，上面写着“黑客攻击”。
接受BuzzFeed新闻采访的Facebook员工指出，公司在执行社区标准方面缺乏一致性和沟通不力，这是一个关键的挫折。5月下旬，在Twitter决定在警告标签后面贴上一篇关于特朗普邮寄选票的误导性帖子后，扎克伯格出现在福克斯新闻(Fox News)上，斥责他的竞争对手试图成为“真相的仲裁者”。
他在接受采访时表示：“我认为，总的来说，私营公司可能不应该--尤其是这些平台公司--不应该处于这样做的境地。”特朗普在Facebook上也做出了同样的虚假声明，Facebook没有对此采取行动。
当周晚些时候，特朗普发表了他的“掠夺”声明，推特(Twitter)主持了这一声明，而Facebook没有，扎克伯格-Facebook公关主管所说的“最终决策者”-为自己的立场进行了辩护。他在5月29日的一篇帖子中写道：“与推特不同的是，我们没有在可能煽动暴力的帖子前放置警告的政策，因为我们认为，如果一篇帖子煽动暴力，无论它是否具有新闻价值，都应该被删除，即使它来自一名政客。”
扎克伯格仅仅用了四天时间就改变了主意。在6月2日的一次全公司会议上，Facebook创始人表示，公司正在考虑为来自世界各国领导人的煽动暴力的帖子添加标签。Recode最先报道了这一评论。三天后，他在Facebook上发布了一篇帖子，宣布“黑人的生命也很重要”，并承诺该公司将审查有关讨论“过度使用警察或国家武力”的内容的政策。
“这些都有什么实质性的影响呢？”一名员工后来在Facebook的Workplace上发问，公开挑战他们的首席执行官。“对审查的承诺没有提供任何实质性的东西。对你来说，有什么改变是有意义的吗？你真的愿意在这里犯错吗？“。
该公司6月18日决定删除特朗普的一则竞选广告，该广告的特点是纳粹用一个三角形符号来识别政治犯，但这并没有让事情变得更好。扎克伯格在广告被撤下几个小时后的全体员工会议上表示，这一行动是一个轻松的呼吁，也是Facebook致力于平等应用其政策的证据。
“从我的角度来看，这个决定并不是特别危险，”他说。“我们在所有这些事情上的立场是，我们希望允许尽可能大的自由表达空间(…)。但如果有东西越界了，不管是谁，我们都会把它拿下。“。
然而，BuzzFeed新闻查看的文件显示，Facebook决定删除特朗普的广告并不像扎克伯格声称的那么简单。事实上，该公司直到面临外部压力，尽管内部员工发出警报，才采取行动。
在广告被移除的前一天早上，Facebook员工之间进行了激烈的讨论，此前至少有九个不同的人表示，他们已经报告了这些内容，但被告知这没有违反公司政策。
“我报告了这件事，并将其称为可能的仇恨言论和暴力威胁。用户体验员工Natalie Troxel在Workplace上写道：“这显然没有违反我们的CS，因为我们的CS从根本上说是坏的。”她指的是公司的社区标准。
凯特琳·沙利文(Kaitlin Sullivan)在LinkedIn上的个人资料显示，她领导着“Facebook内容政策团队的美洲分公司”。她回复说，这则广告仍在评估中，“没有更多上下文的三角关系显然没有违反那里的信件。”她补充说，将该公司的流程描述为“支离破碎”是没有“帮助”的。
“我坚持我说过的话……。一位世界领导人在我们的平台上推广使用露骨的纳粹图像的内容，“Troxel回应说。
另一名员工补充说：“这一功能在我们的平台上保留了这么长时间，这一事实令人担忧。”
一名Facebook员工因害怕报复而匿名接受BuzzFeed新闻采访时表示，他们“大吃一惊”，这些广告没有立即被裁定为违法。这位人士说：“Facebook内部有一种真正的文化，那就是想当然地认为自己的意图是好的。”“对我来说，这是一个你不能对一个可能是纳粹形象的符号抱有良好意图的情况。”他们还感到困扰的是，Facebook是在收到华盛顿邮报的问题后才对这则广告采取行动的-在员工标记这则广告超过12个小时后。
这位匿名员工表示：“如果没有媒体压力，Facebook员工对Workplace上的那些帖子和帖子的参与程度超出正常水平，看起来肯定不会做出这样的决定。”
这起事件是Facebook拒绝对违规内容采取行动，但在受到公众批评后才改变主意的又一个例子。这再一次引发了人们的疑问，即决策是如何做出的，政策是始终如一地实施，还是针对负面宣传或政治担忧做出反应。
前Facebook选举广告诚信主管艾森斯塔特(Eisenstat)表示，该公司未能对特朗普的广告迅速采取行动，也未能缓和他的“掠夺”帖子，这进一步证明，政治考虑和外部压力正在影响政策决定。
她说：“他们把政治考量放在执行政策上，严格遵守法律。”“我可以在那里不止一次地说，(华盛顿)政策团队不止一次参与了上诉和决定，这些上诉和决定清楚地表明，我们如何执行政策是有政治考虑的。”
本月早些时候，白人民族主义的Facebook页面“白人幸福澳大利亚”(White Welling Australia)的一则广告上演了一个相关的场景。这则广告宣称：“白人只占世界人口的8%，所以如果所有白人占多数的国家都充斥着非白人，你就永远消灭了白人儿童。”
直到BuzzFeed新闻在7月8日星期三联系到Facebook之后，Facebook才删除了这则广告，并阻止该页面未来播放付费内容。但这一举措也是在一名公司员工标记了这则广告并被告知它没有违规之后采取的。
BuzzFeed新闻发布后，Facebook项目经理马修·布伦南(Matthew Brennan)在Workplace上写道：“我报告了这个页面和帖子，被告知它没有达到内容违规的门槛。”“毫无疑问，这是一个白人至上组织，有问题的帖子并没有试图掩盖这一事实。”
Brennan在他的News Feed上看到了这则广告，他说，澳大利亚的朋友和家人周末提出了对这则广告的担忧，这让他感到沮丧，因为“对此无能为力”。
“在Facebook工作并不能让我感到自豪，”布伦南总结道。
另一名员工回应说：“我从我的妻子、朋友和家人那里得到了同样的信息。”“这些决定将站在历史的错误一边。”
最终，Facebook删除了这则广告，并阻止白人民族主义页面播放未来的广告。然而，这个页面仍然存在于社交网络上。
当地时间2019年10月23日，美国华盛顿特区，雷伯恩众议院办公楼内，扎克伯格在众议院金融服务委员会作证。
在他的告别视频中，王健林指责扎克伯格在6月初的一次会议上对特朗普“抢劫”帖子的决定进行了解释，并指责扎克伯格给员工提供了毒气和“诱饵和开关”。扎克伯格问道，为什么扎克伯格只谈论特朗普的言论是否符合公司规则，而不是修改允许最初可能伤害人的威胁的政策。
“看着这一切，感觉就像有人在慢慢地把我脚下的地毯换掉，”王说。“他们把对道德、正义或规范的担忧换成了对一致性和逻辑性的担忧，好像很明显，‘一致性’才是最重要的。”
即将离任的工程师所说的话呼应了诸如颜色变化h等民权组织的观点。

note_en:
On July 1, Max Wang, a Boston-based software engineer who was leaving Facebook after more than seven years, shared a video on the company’s internal discussion board that was meant to serve as a warning.
 “I think Facebook is hurting people at scale,” he wrote in a note accompanying the video. “If you think so too, maybe give this a watch.”
 Most employees on their way out of the “Mark Zuckerberg production” typically post photos of their company badges along with farewell notes thanking their colleagues. Wang opted for a clip of himself speaking directly to the camera. What followed was a 24-minute clear-eyed hammering of Facebook’s leadership and decision-making over the previous year.
 The video was a distillation of months of internal strife, protest, and departures that followed the company’s decision to leave untouched a post from President Donald Trump that seemingly called for violence against people protesting  the police killing of George Floyd. And while Wang’s message wasn’t necessarily unique, his assessment of the company’s ongoing failure to protect its users — an evaluation informed by his lengthy tenure at the company — provided one of the most stunningly pointed rebukes of Facebook to date.
 “We are failing,” he said, criticizing Facebook’s leaders for catering to political concerns at the expense of real-world harm. “And what's worse, we have enshrined that failure in our policies.”
    While external criticisms of Facebook, which has roughly 3 billion users across its various social platforms, have persisted since the run-up to the 2016 presidential election, they’ve rarely sparked wide-scale dissent inside the social media giant. As it weathered one scandal after another —  Russian election interference,  Cambridge Analytica,  Rohingya genocide in Myanmar — over the past three and a half years, Facebook’s stock price rose and it continued to recruit and retain top talent. In spite of the  occasional internal dustup, employees generally felt the company was doing more good than harm. At the very least, they avoided publicly airing their grievances.
    “This time, our response feels different,” wrote Facebook engineer Dan Abramov in a June 26 post on Workplace, the company’s internal communications platform. “I’ve taken some [paid time off] to refocus, but I can’t shake the feeling that the company leadership has betrayed the trust my colleagues and I have placed in them.”
 Messages like those from Wang and Abramov illustrate how Facebook’s handling of the president’s often divisive posts has caused a sea change in its ranks and led to a crisis of confidence in leadership, according to interviews with current and former employees and dozens of documents obtained by BuzzFeed News. The documents — which include company discussion threads, employee survey results, and recordings of Zuckerberg — reveal that the company was slow to take down ads with white nationalist and Nazi content reported by its own employees. They demonstrate how the company’s public declarations about supporting racial justice causes are at odds with policies forbidding Facebookers from using company resources to support political matters. They show Zuckerberg being publicly accused of misleading his employees. Above all, they portray a fracturing company culture.
 Frustrated and angry, employees are now challenging Zuckerberg and leadership at companywide meetings, staging virtual walkouts, and questioning if their work is making the world a better place. The turmoil has reached a point where Facebook's CEO recently threatened to fire employees who “bully” their colleagues.
 As it heads into a US presidential election where its every move will be dissected and analyzed, the social network is facing unprecedented internal dissent as employees worry that the company is wittingly or unwittingly exerting political influence on content decisions related to Trump, and fear that Facebook is undermining democracy.
    Yaël Eisenstat, Facebook's former election ads integrity lead, said the employees’ concerns reflect her experience at the company, which she believes is on a dangerous path heading into the election.
 “All of these steps are leading up to a situation where, come November, a portion of Facebook users will not trust the outcome of the election because they have been bombarded with messages on Facebook preparing them to not trust it,” she told BuzzFeed News.
 She said the company’s policy team in Washington, DC, led by Joel Kaplan, sought to unduly influence decisions made by her team, and the company’s recent failure to take appropriate action on posts from President Trump shows employees are right to be upset and concerned.
 “These were very clear examples that didn't just upset me, they upset Facebook’s employees, they upset the entire civil rights community, they upset Facebook’s advertisers. If you still refuse to listen to all those voices, then you're proving that your decision-making is being guided by some other voice,” she said.
 Do you work at Facebook or another technology company? We'd love to hear from you. Reach out at   ryan.mac@buzzfeed.com  or  via one of our   tip line channels .
 In a broad statement responding to a list of questions for this story, a Facebook spokesperson said the company has a rigorous policy process and is transparent with employees about how decisions are made.
 “Content decisions at Facebook are made based on our best, most even, application of the public policies as written. It will always be the case that groups of people, even employees, see these decisions as inconsistent; that’s the nature of applying policies broadly,” the spokesperson said. “That’s why we’ve implemented a rigorous process of both consulting with outside experts when adopting new policies as well as soliciting feedback from employees and why we’ve created an independent oversight board to appeal content policy decisions on Facebook.”
 In his note, Abramov, who’s worked at the social network for four years, compared Facebook to a nuclear power plant. Facebook, unlike traditional media sources, can generate “social energy” at a scale never seen before, he said.
 “But even getting small details wrong can lead to disastrous consequences,” he wrote. “Social media has enough power to damage the fabric of our society. If you think that’s an overstatement, you aren’t paying attention.”
    On May 28, as protests against police brutality raged in Minneapolis and around the country, President Donald Trump posted identical messages to his Facebook and Twitter accounts, which have a collective 114 million followers.
 “Just spoke to Governor Tim Walz and told him that the Military is with him all the way,” the president wrote that night. “Any difficulty and we will assume control but, when the looting starts, the shooting starts.”
 Within a matter of hours, Twitter placed Trump’s post behind a warning label, noting it violated its rules around glorifying violence. Meanwhile, Facebook did nothing. It had decided that the phrase “when the looting starts, the shooting starts” — which has  historical ties to racially oppressive police violence — did not constitute a violation of its terms of service.
 In explaining the decision the next day, Zuckerberg said that while he had a “visceral negative reaction” to the post, Facebook policies allowed for “discussion around the state use of force.” Moreover, he argued that, in spite of the phrase’s historical context, it was possible that it could have been interpreted to mean the president was simply warning that looting could lead to violence. ( Axios later reported that Zuckerberg had personally called Trump the day after the post.)
 Employees, already angered by the company’s failure to take action against  a post from Trump earlier that May containing mail-in ballot misinformation, revolted. In a Workplace group called “Let’s Fix Facebook (the company),” which has about 10,000 members, an employee started a poll asking colleagues whether they agreed “with our leadership’s decisions this week regarding voting misinformation and posts that may be considered to be inciting violence.” About 1,000 respondents said the company had made the wrong decision on both posts, more than 20 times the number of responses for the third-highest answer, “I’m not sure.”
    “I don't know what to do, but I know doing nothing is not acceptable,” Jason Stirman, a Facebook design manager at Facebook,  wrote on Twitter that weekend, one of a lengthy stream of dissenting voices. “I'm a FB employee that completely disagrees with Mark's decision to do nothing about Trump's recent posts, which clearly incite violence. I'm not alone inside of FB. There isn't a neutral position on racism.”
 The following Monday, hundreds of employees — most working remotely due to the company’s  coronavirus policies — changed their Workplace avatars to a white and black fist and called out sick in a digital walkout.
 As Facebook grappled with yet another public relations crisis, employee morale plunged. Worker satisfaction metrics — measured by “MicroPulse” surveys that are taken by hundreds of employees every week — fell sharply after the ruling on Trump’s "looting" post, according to data obtained by BuzzFeed News.
 On June 1, the day of the walkout, about 45% of employees said they agreed with the statement that Facebook was making the world better — down about 25 percentage points from the week before. That same day, Facebook’s internal surveys showed that around 44% of employees were confident in “Facebook leadership leading the company in the right direction” — a 30 percentage point drop from May 25. Responses to that question have stayed around that lower mark as of earlier this month, according to data seen by BuzzFeed News.
  Two charts recreated from by BuzzFeed News show employee satisfaction metrics measured by Facebook's "MicroPulse" surveys.
  Culturally, Facebook has become increasingly divided, with some company loyalists pushing the idea that a silent majority supported the call made on the Trump post.
 On Blind, a forum app that requires work emails for people to anonymously discuss their employer, some unnamed employees demanded that those involved in the walkout be fired. One thread focused specifically on Jason Toff, a director of product management at Facebook, who had  tweeted following the Trump decision that he was “not proud of how we’re showing up.”
 “His major responsibility is to acquire top talent and motivate his team, and that’s impossible to do when you publicly share that you aren’t proud of where you work at,” wrote one Blind user on a thread titled “Fire Jason Toff.”
    Other anonymous Facebook employees vilified May Zhou, a software engineer who had asked at an all-hands meeting how many Black employees had been involved in the Trump "looting" post decision. They called her "disrespectful" and a “sjw” — pejorative shorthand for “social justice warrior.” (In response to Zhou’s question, Zuckerberg said only one Black employee in the company’s Austin office had been consulted and that Facebook’s political speech policies needed tweaking.)
 Toff declined to speak for this story. Zhou did not return a request for comment.
 This ongoing contention and erosion of Facebook’s culture has infuriated Zuckerberg. In a June 11 live Q&A with employees, he pointedly addressed it.
 “I've been very worried about … the level of disrespect and, in some cases, of vitriol that a lot of people in our internal community are directing towards each other as part of these debates,” he said. “If you're bullying your fellow colleagues into taking a position on something, then we will  fire you.”
  Facebook employees walk past a sign reading "hack" at Facebook's corporate headquarters campus in Menlo Park, California, Oct. 23, 2019.
  Facebook employees who spoke to BuzzFeed News pointed to the company’s lack of consistency and poor communication around enforcement of its community standards as a key frustration. In late May, following a decision by Twitter to place a misleading post about mail-in ballots from Trump behind a warning label, Zuckerberg  appeared on Fox News to chastise his competitor for trying to be an “arbiter of truth.”
 “I think in general, private companies probably shouldn’t be — or especially these platform companies — shouldn’t be in the position of doing that,” he said in the interview. Trump had made the same false claim on Facebook, which took no action against it.
 Later that week, when Trump made his “looting” statement and Twitter moderated it and Facebook did not, Zuckerberg — the “ultimate decision-maker” according to  Facebook’s head of communications — defended his position. “Unlike Twitter, we do not have a policy of putting a warning in front of posts that may incite violence because we believe that if a post incites violence, it should be removed regardless of whether it is newsworthy, even if it comes from a politician,”  he wrote in a post on May 29.
 It took just four days for Zuckerberg to change his mind. In comments at a companywide meeting on June 2 that were  first reported by Recode, Facebook’s founder said the company was considering adding labels to posts from world leaders that incite violence. He followed that up with  a Facebook post three days later, in which he declared “Black lives matter,” and made promises that the company would review policies on content discussing “excessive use of police or state force.”
    “What material effect does any of this have?” one employee later asked on Facebook’s Workplace, openly challenging their CEO. “Commitments to review offer nothing material. Has anything changed for you in a meaningful way? Are you at all willing to be wrong here?”
 The company’s June 18 decision to  remove a Trump campaign ad that featured a triangle symbol used by Nazis to identify political prisoners didn’t make things any better. Speaking at an all-hands meeting hours after the ads were taken down, Zuckerberg said the action was an easy call and evidence of Facebook’s commitment to applying its policies equally.
 “This decision was not a particularly close call from my perspective,” he said. “Our position on all this stuff is we want to allow as wide open an aperture of free expression as possible … but if something is over the line, no matter who it is, we will take it down.”
 Documents reviewed by BuzzFeed News, however, reveal Facebook's decision to remove the Trump ads was not as simple as Zuckerberg claimed. In fact, the company failed to act on them until after it faced outside pressure, and despite internal alerts from its own employees.
 On the morning before the ads were removed, a heated discussion took place among Facebook employees after at least nine different people said they had reported the content but were told it did not violate company policy.
 “I reported it and called it out as both possible hate speech and threat of violence. This is apparently not a violation of our CS because our CS are fundamentally broken,” Natalie Troxel, a user experience employee wrote on Workplace, referencing the company’s community standards.
 Kaitlin Sullivan, whose LinkedIn profile says she leads “the Americas branch of Facebook’s content policy team,” replied, saying the ad was still being evaluated and that “the triangle without any more context doesn’t clearly violate the letter there.” It wasn’t “helpful” to describe the company’s process as broken, she added.
    “I stand by what I said ... A world leader is promoting content on our platform that uses explicit Nazi imagery,” Troxel responded.
 “The fact that this has even remained on our platform for this long is troubling,” another employee added.
  A Facebook employee who spoke to BuzzFeed News anonymously for fear of retaliation said they were “flabbergasted” the ads weren't immediately ruled violative. “There’s a real culture within Facebook to assume good intent,” this person said. “To me, this was a case where you cannot assume good intent for a symbol that  could be Nazi imagery.” They also were bothered that Facebook took action on the ad only after receiving questions from  the Washington Post — more than 12 hours after it had been flagged by employees.
 “It certainly looks like a decision would not have been made the way it was had there not been media pressure and a larger than normal involvement of Facebook employees on those posts and threads on Workplace,” the anonymous employee said.
 The incident was yet another example of Facebook declining to act on violative content only to change its mind following public criticism. And once again, it raised questions about how decisions are made, and whether policies are applied consistently or  in reaction to negative publicity or political concerns.
 Eisenstat, the former Facebook election ads integrity lead, said the company’s failure to act quickly on the Trump ads, and to moderate his “looting” post, are further evidence that political considerations and outside pressure are influencing policy decisions.
 “They put political considerations over enforcing their policies to the letter of the law,” she said. “I can say for my time there that more than once the [Washington] policy team weighed in on appeals and decisions that made it clear there was a political consideration factoring into how we were enforcing our policy.”
    A related scenario played out earlier this month over  an ad run by a white nationalist Facebook page, “White Wellbeing Australia.” “White people make up just 8% of the world's population so if you flood all white majority countries with nonwhites you eliminate white children forever,” the ad proclaimed.
 Facebook removed the ad and prevented the page from running paid content in the future only after being contacted by BuzzFeed News on Wednesday, July 8. But that move too came after a company employee flagged the ad and was told it wasn’t violative.
 “I reported the page and the post and was told that it doesn’t meet the threshold for a content violation,” Facebook project manager Matthew Brennan wrote on Workplace after the publication of BuzzFeed News’ story. “There is no doubt this is a white supremacy group and the post in question is not trying to hide that fact.”
 Brennan, who saw the ad appear in his News Feed, said friends and family in Australia raised concerns about the ad over the weekend, leaving him frustrated that “there was nothing that could be done about it.”
 “It wasn’t exactly filling me with pride to work at Facebook,” Brennan concluded.
 “I get the same from my wife, friends, and family,” another employee responded. “These decisions are going to be on the wrong side of history.”
  In the end, Facebook removed the ad and prevented the white nationalist page from running future ads. The page, however, continues to exist on the social network.
  Zuckerberg testifies before the House Financial Services Committee in the Rayburn House Office Building on Capitol Hill in Washington, DC, Oct. 23, 2019.
  In his farewell video, Wang accused Zuckerberg of "gaslighting" employees and a “bait and switch” during an early June meeting in which the Facebook CEO explained the decision on the Trump “looting” post. Why was Zuckerberg only talking about whether Trump’s comments fit the company’s rules, and not about fixing policies that allowed for threats that could hurt people in the first place, he asked.
 “Watching this just felt like someone was sort of slowly swapping out the rug from under my feet,” Wang said. “They were swapping concerns about morals or justice or norms with this concern about consistency and logic, as if it were obviously the case that ‘consistency’ is what mattered most.”

title_en: Facebook's employees reckon with the social network they've built

note:
华盛顿(CNN Business)Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)周四表示，Twitter正在积极探索从用户那里赚钱的其他方式，包括考虑订阅模式。此举正值推特核心广告业务大幅下滑之际。
多尔西在讨论该公司第二季度收益业绩的投资者电话会议上告诉分析师，今年你可能会看到各种方法的一些考验。多尔西说，当我们要求消费者为Twitter的某些方面付费时，他有一个非常高的门槛，但他证实，该公司正寻求在非常、非常早期的探索阶段实现收入来源的多元化。
本月早些时候，Twitter发布了一个职位空缺，专注于打造代号为“鹰头鹰”(Gryphon)的订阅平台，随后有关Twitter提供付费服务的传言甚嚣尘上。Twitter的股价当时飙升，表明投资者对该公司寻找新的收入来源有兴趣。
与其竞争对手社交网络一样，Twitter一直专注于提供免费服务，并通过允许品牌向其数百万用户投放广告来赚钱。
多尔西表示：我们希望确保任何新的收入线都能与我们的广告业务相辅相成。我们确实认为，在这个世界里，订阅是互补的，商业是互补的，帮助人们管理付费墙……。我们认为是互补的。
Twitter的增长计划正受到密切关注，因为许多广告商因流感大流行而收缩。周四，Twitter公布第二季度广告收入为5.62亿美元，与去年同期相比下降了23%。
该公司还受到了参与广告抵制社交媒体的广告商的打击，这与全国范围内的种族正义抗议活动有关。但Twitter高管拒绝透露抵制活动对Twitter业务产生了多大影响。
在Twitter发布财报之前，多尔西将其描述为艰难的一周。在这一周里，Twitter争先恐后地应对一场大规模黑客攻击，这起攻击泄露了众多已验证账户，包括巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)、乔·拜登(Joe Biden)、埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)和杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)的账户。
在财报公布前夕，Twitter宣布，作为安全事件的一部分，数十个账户--包括一名荷兰民选官员--可能被黑客窃取了他们的直接消息。
多尔西在周四的电话会议上为上周的大规模安全漏洞道歉，称我们未能履行公司的安全义务。
他说：我们对这次安全事件感到很难过。安全没有终点。这是一个不断的迭代。我们将继续超越这里，因为我们将继续保护我们的系统，并继续与外部公司和执法部门合作。
note_en:
Washington (CNN Business) Twitter  (  TWTR) is actively exploring additional ways to make money from its users, including by considering a subscription model, CEO Jack Dorsey said Thursday. The move comes as Twitter suffers a sharp decline in its core advertising business.
 "You will likely see some tests this year" of various approaches, Dorsey told analysts on an investor call held to discuss the company's second quarter earnings results. Dorsey said he has "a really high bar for when we would ask consumers to pay for aspects of Twitter," but confirmed that the company is seeking to diversify its sources of revenue in what are "very, very early phases of exploring."
 Earlier this month, rumors flared about a paid Twitter option after the company  posted a job opening focused on building a subscription platform codenamed "Gryphon." Twitter's stock surged at the time, signaling investor appetite for the company to find new revenue streams.
    Like its rival social networks, Twitter has focused on offering a free service and making money by allowing brands to target ads to its millions of users.
  "We want to make sure any new line of revenue is complementary to our advertising business," Dorsey said. "We do think there is a world where subscription is complementary, where commerce is complementary, where helping people manage paywalls ... we think is complementary."
 Twitter's growth plans are under close scrutiny as many advertisers pull back due to the pandemic. On Thursday, Twitter reported second-quarter ad revenues of $562 million, a 23% decrease compared to the same quarter a year ago.
 The company has also been hit by advertisers participating in an  ad boycott of social media, linked to the nationwide racial justice protests. But Twitter executives declined to say how much of an impact the boycott has had on Twitter's business.
 Twitter's earnings report follows what Dorsey described as a "tough week" in which the company scrambled to address a massive hack that compromised numerous verified accounts, including those of Barack Obama, Joe Biden, Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos.
 On the eve of its earnings results, Twitter announced that  dozens of accounts -- including one elected official in The Netherlands -- may have had their direct messages accessed by hackers as part of the security incident.
   Dorsey apologized on the conference call Thursday for last week's massive security breach, saying "we fell behind" on the company's security obligations.
 "We feel terrible about the security incident," he said. "Security doesn't have an end point. It's a constant iteration ... We will continue to go above and beyond here as we continue to secure our systems and as we continue to work with external firms and law enforcement."
title: 由于广告收入大幅下降，Twitter正在考虑订阅选择
title_en: Twitter is looking at subscription options as ad revenue drops sharply

周四，一枚重型长征五号火箭在海南岛的一个海滨航天港发射后，推动了中国首次向这颗红色星球登陆火星的任务，这是预计本月将离开地球的三个国际火星探测器中的第二个。
上午12点41分，中国天文一号飞船从中国南部海南省文昌航天发射中心发射升空，开启了为期近七个月的旅程。美国东部夏令时(格林尼治时间0441GMT；下午12：41。北京时间)在长征五号火箭上，这是中国库存中最重的发射器。
发射场附近业余观众直播的视频显示，长征五号火箭正在爬离文昌航天港。10个以煤油和液氢为燃料的火箭发动机将187英尺(57米)高的长征5号送入阳光明媚的正午天空，在非官方的在线视频中，火箭很快从人们的视线中消失了。
中国官方媒体没有提前直播这次任务，也没有提前公布确切的发射时间，但空域和海事告示警告飞行员和水手避开下落区，暗示长征五号计划于周四发射升空。
一旦1.1万磅(5吨)的天文一号航天器被长征五号第二级送入火星轨道，中国当局就解除了对发射的新闻封锁。中国国家航天局证实，长征五号火箭在发射约36分钟后，将天文一号送入了通往火星的正确轨道。
中国航天科技集团公司，中国航天计划的国有主承包商，在一份声明中宣布发射成功。
天文一号计划在经过七个月的航行后，于明年2月抵达火星。如果成功，这将是中国第一个进入另一颗行星轨道的探测器。
两三个月后，天文一号轨道器将释放一个着陆器进入火星大气层，目标是在火星北半球的广阔平原乌托邦普兰蒂亚(Utopia Planitia)进行受控着陆。一旦到达地面，着陆器将放下一个坡道，一辆529磅(240公斤)的月球车将驶入地面。
如果中国按计划实现这些壮举，他们将使中国成为继苏联和美国之后第三个在火星上软着陆的国家，也是第二个在这个红色星球上驾驶机器人漫游车的国家。
周四由长征五号火箭进行的看似无懈可击的发射，使中国最强大的发射器在五次飞行后创造了80%的成功率记录。长征五号在2017年的第二次试飞中失败，但现在已经连续三次成功。
天文一号是继一系列日益复杂的机器人登月探险之后，中国在太阳系探索方面的下一次飞跃。
最近，中国已经在月球上着陆了两辆月球车，其中包括第一辆探索月球远侧表面的月球车。中国下一次月球任务名为嫦娥五号，计划于今年晚些时候乘坐长征五号火箭发射，执行从月球返回样本的任务。
这将是俄罗斯第二次尝试使用机器人探测器到达火星，此前俄罗斯失败的福布斯-格朗特(Phobos-Grunt)任务将英火一号轨道器作为搭载有效载荷发射后搁浅在地球轨道上。
中国火星探测计划首席科学家万卫星在本月发表在科学期刊《自然·天文学》(Nature Astronomy)上的一篇论文中写道：“得益于中国探月计划的工程遗产，中国的国家战略将火星定为行星探测的下一个目标。”中国的第一次火星任务被命名为天文一号，目标是在一次任务中完成绕轨、着陆和漫游。
中国官员在4月份宣布了国家行星任务的天文名称。根据中国国家航天局(CNSA)的说法，天文这个名字来自中国古代诗人屈原的作品，意思是“追求天堂的真理”。
中国国家航空航天局在一份声明中说：“该国的第一个火星探测器将对火星土壤、地质结构、环境、大气和水进行科学调查。”
在2月份到达火星后，天文一号航天器将初步进入围绕这颗红色星球的长周期捕获轨道。轨道飞行器最终将在火星两极上空165英里(265公里)到近7500英里(12000公里)的范围内围绕火星飞行一圈。
最快在明年4月，着陆器和月球车模块将从轨道器上分离出来，开始通过火星大气层下降。来自轨道的雷达探测表明，在天文一号着陆器瞄准的乌托邦普兰蒂亚地区，存在一个冰库，其水量与五大湖中最大的苏必利尔湖(Lake Superior)相当。
天文一号轨道器的设计运行时间至少为火星一年，即在地球上运行约两年。中国官员说，这辆太阳能漫游车配备了六个轮子用于移动，预期寿命至少为90天。
中国科学家表示，天文一号任务将对火星进行全球调查，测量土壤和岩石成分，寻找埋藏水冰的迹象，并研究火星磁层和大气。轨道器和月球车还将观察火星的天气，并探测火星的内部结构。
天文1号月球车被包裹在隔热罩内，以便火热地降落到火星表面。从轨道母舰释放后，着陆器将进入红色星球的大气层，展开降落伞，然后发射刹车火箭减速着陆。
已故中国火星计划首席科学家万在“自然·天文学”(Nature Astronomy)上写道：“天文一号将在第一次尝试中就将月球车送入轨道、着陆和释放，并与轨道器协调观测。”“从来没有以这种方式执行过行星任务。如果成功，将标志着一项重大的技术突破。
从科学上讲，天文一号是调查火星地貌、地质、矿物学、空间环境和水土冰川分布的最全面的任务。
火星车的探地雷达将是首批到达火星表面的科学仪器之一。NASA的“毅力月球车”携带着一台类似的仪器，可以扫描火星地壳的亚表层，寻找水冰沉积物。
天文一号是一个由中国牵头的项目，但来自几个国家的科学家和支持团队已经同意为这项任务提供援助。
来自法国天体物理与计划学研究所(IRAP)的科学家为天文1号月球车上的激光诱导击穿光谱仪器做出了贡献。
在法国国家空间研究中心(CNES)的支持下，法国科学家向中国同行提供了光谱技术方面的指导，这种技术使用激光击打针头大小的岩石部分，并使用光谱仪分析激光与岩石表面相互作用产生的等离子体发出的光。
IRAP的行星科学家艾格尼斯·科桑(Agnes Cousin)表示，法国和中国科学家之间的讨论旨在“最大限度地提高天文一号月球车产生的数据质量”。她与中国研究人员合作开发了天文一号月球车的仪器。
来自同一研究所的法国科学家帮助开发了NASA好奇号月球车上的ChemCam仪器和将于7月30日发射的NASA Persistance火星漫游车上的SuperCam有效载荷。ChemCam和SuperCam使用与天文1号月球车相同的激光诱导击穿光谱技术。
来自法国的研究人员提供了一个北纬校准目标，用于在天文1号月球车上飞行。它类似于NASA好奇号月球车上的一个装置，用来校准ChemCam的测量结果，方法是将仪器对准一个具有已知成分的目标-比如岩石诺利石。
NASA毅力月球车上的SuperCam仪器将把一种不同类型的岩石作为校准目标，但堂兄表示，她在法国实验室的科学家们仍然能够交叉校准好奇号、毅力号和天文1号月球车的测量结果。
奥地利科学院空间研究所的科学家协助开发了天文一号轨道器上的磁强计，并帮助校准了飞行仪器。
阿根廷拥有一台中国拥有的深空跟踪天线，发射后将用于与天文一号通信。欧洲航天局已经同意在自己的全球深空跟踪站网络上为天文一号提供通信时间，并帮助探测器在火星之旅中导航。
周四，天文一号火星任务的发射距离阿联酋开发的希望火星探测器发射不到四天，距离美国国家航空航天局(NASA)的毅力火星漫游车计划发射还有一周的时间。
行星不断变化的位置只允许任务每隔26个月左右从地球直接前往火星一次。火星发射窗口于今年7月中旬开启，并延长至8月中旬。
虽然NASA和美国科学家正在协助阿联酋的希望火星轨道器，但NASA在中国的天文一号任务中没有这样的角色。美国国家航空航天局(NASA)的深空网络为众多美国和国际太空探测器提供跟踪和通信覆盖，但尚未被征召来支持天文一号的火星之旅。
取而代之的是，中国正在使用自己的跟踪天线和欧空局的全球地面站网络相结合。
布里登斯汀在推特上写道：“通过今天的发射，中国正在加入火星国际科学探险家的行列。”“美国、欧洲、俄罗斯、印度以及很快的阿联酋都将欢迎你们来到火星，开启令人兴奋的科学发现之年。平安出行天文一号！“。

  A heavy-lift Long March 5 rocket propelled China’s first Mars landing mission toward the Red Planet on Thursday after launching from a seaside spaceport on Hainan Island, the second of three international Mars probes expected to depart planet Earth this month.
 Kicking off a nearly seven-month journey, China’s Tianwen 1 spacecraft lifted off from the Wenchang Space Launch Center in southern China’s Hainan province at 12:41 a.m. EDT (0441 GMT; 12:41 p.m. Beijing time) on top of a Long March 5 rocket, the heaviest launcher in the country’s inventory.
 A live video feed streamed by amateur spectators near the launch site showed the Long March 5 rocket climbing away from the Wenchang spaceport. Ten rocket engines fueled by kerosene and liquid hydrogen powered the 187-foot-tall (57-meter) Long March 5 into a sunny midday sky, and the rocket quickly receded from view in the unofficial online video feed.
 Chinese state media did not broadcast the mission live or publicize the exact launch time in advance, but airspace and maritime notices warning pilots and sailors to steer clear of downrange drop zones suggested the Long March 5 was scheduled to lift off Thursday.
 Chinese authorities lifted the news blackout on the launch once the 11,000-pound (5-metric ton) Tianwen 1 spacecraft was injected onto a trajectory toward Mars by the Long March 5’s second stage. The China National Space Administration confirmed the Long March 5 rocket placed Tianwen 1 on the proper course toward Mars about 36 minutes after launch.
 The China Aerospace Science and Technology Corp., the government-owned prime contractor for China’s space program, declared the launch a success in a statement.
 Tianwen 1 is scheduled to arrive at Mars next February after a seven-month voyage. If successful, the mission will be China’s first probe to enter orbit around another planet.
 Two-to-three months later, the Tianwen 1 orbiter will release a lander to enter the Martian atmosphere and aim for a controlled touchdown in Utopia Planitia, a broad plain in Mars’s northern hemisphere. Once on the surface, the lander will lower a ramp and a 529-pound (240-kilogram) rover will drive onto the surface.
 If China pulls off those feats according to plan, they will make China the third country to perform a soft landing on Mars — after the Soviet Union and the United States — and the second country to drive a robotic rover on the Red Planet.
   The seemingly flawless launch Thursday by the Long March 5 rocket gives China’s most powerful launcher an 80 percent success record after five flights. The Long March 5 failed on its second test flight in 2017, but has now logged three consecutive successes.
 Tianwen 1 is China’s next leap in solar system exploration after a series of progressively complex robotic expeditions to the moon.
 Most recently, China has landed two rovers on the moon, including the first to explore the surface of the lunar far side. The next Chinese lunar mission, named Chang’e 5, is scheduled for launch on a Long March 5 rocket late this year on a mission to return samples from the moon.
  It will be the country’s second attempt to reach Mars with a robotic probe, following the Yinghuo 1 orbiter, which was stranded in Earth orbit after launch as a piggyback payload on Russia’s failed Phobos-Grunt mission.
 “Benefiting from the engineering heritage of China’s lunar exploration program, the Chinese national strategy set Mars as the next target for planetary exploration,” wrote Wan Weixing, chief scientist of China’s Mars exploration program, in a paper published this month by the science journal Nature Astronomy. “China’s first Mars mission is named Tianwen 1, and aims to complete orbiting, landing and roving in one mission.”
  Chinese officials announced the Tianwen name for the country’s planetary missions in April. The name Tianwen comes from the work of ancient Chinese poet Qu Yuan, meaning “quest for heavenly truth,” according to the China National Space Administration, or CNSA, the country’s space agency.
 “The country’s first Martian probe will conduct scientific investigations about the Martian soil, geological structure, environment, atmosphere, as well as water,” CNSA said in a statement.
 After reaching Mars in February, the Tianwen 1 spacecraft will initially enter a long-period capture orbit around the Red Planet. The orbiter will eventually settle in a loop around Mars ranging between 165 miles (265 kilometers) and nearly 7,500 miles (12,000 kilometers) over the Martian poles.
 As soon as next April, the lander and rover modules will detach from the orbiter to begin a descent through the Martian atmosphere. Radar soundings from orbit have indicated the presence of a reservoir of ice containing as much water as Lake Superior, the largest of the Great Lakes, in the Utopia Planitia region targeted by Tianwen 1’s lander.
  The Tianwen 1 orbiter is designed to operate for at least one Martian year, or about two years on Earth. The solar-powered rover, fitted with six wheels for mobility, has a life expectancy of at least 90 days, Chinese officials said.
 Chinese scientists say the Tianwen 1 mission will perform a global survey of Mars, measuring soil and rock composition, searching for signs of buried water ice, and studying the Martian magnetosphere and atmosphere. The orbiter and rover will also observe Martian weather and probe Mars’s internal structure.
   The Tianwen 1 rover is cocooned inside a heat shield for a fiery descent to the Martian surface. After releasing from the orbiter mothership, the lander will enter the Red Planet’s atmosphere, deploy a parachute, then fire a braking rocket to slow down for landing.
 “Tianwen 1 is going to orbit, land and release a rover all on the very first try, and coordinate observations with an orbiter,” Wan, the late chief scientist for China’s Mars program, wrote in Nature Astronomy. “No planetary missions have ever been implemented in this way. If successful, it would signify a major technical breakthrough.
 “Scientifically, Tianwen 1 is the most comprehensive mission to investigate the Martian morphology, geology, mineralogy, space environment, and soil and water-ice distribution.”
   The rover’s ground-penetrating radar would be one of the first science instruments of its kind to reach the surface of Mars. NASA’s Perseverance rover carries a comparable instrument to scan subsurface layers of the Martian crust in search of water ice deposits.
 Tianwen 1 is a Chinese-led project, but scientists and support teams from several countries have agreed to provide assistance on the mission.
 Scientists from the Institut de Recherche en Astrophysique et Planétologie, or IRAP, in France contributed to a Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy instrument on the Tianwen 1 rover.
 French scientists, with support from the French space agency CNES, provided guidance to their Chinese counterparts on the spectroscopy technique, which uses a laser to zap a pinhead-size portion of a rock, and a spectrometer to analyze the light given off by plasma generated by the laser’s interaction with the rock’s surface.
   The discussions between French and Chinese scientists were intended to “maximize the quality of the data” produced by the Tianwen 1 rover, according to Agnes Cousin, a planetary scientist at IRAP who worked with Chinese researchers developing the rover’s instruments.
 French scientists from the same research institute helped develop the ChemCam instrument on NASA’s Curiosity rover and the SuperCam payload set for launch July 30 on NASA’s Perseverance Mars rover. ChemCam and SuperCam use the same Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy technique as the Tianwen 1 rover.
 Researchers from France provided a norite calibration target to fly on the Tianwen 1 rover. It’s similar to a unit on NASA’s Curiosity rover used to calibrate ChemCam’s measurements by turning the instrument on a target — like the rock norite — with a known composition.
 The SuperCam instrument on NASA’s Perseverance rover will us a different type rock as a calibration target, but Cousin said scientists at her lab in France will still be able to cross-calibrate measurements from Curiosity, Perseverance, and the Tianwen 1 rover.
 Scientists from the Space Research Institute at the Austrian Academy of Sciences assisted in the development of the magnetometer on the Tianwen 1 orbiter and helped calibrate the flight instrument.
 Argentina is home to a Chinese-owned deep space tracking antenna that will be used to communicate with Tianwen 1 after launch. The European Space Agency has agreed to provide communications time for Tianwen 1 on its own worldwide network of deep space tracking stations, and help with the probe’s navigation on the journey to Mars.
  The launch of the Tianwen 1 Mars mission Thursday occurred less than four days after the launch of the Hope Mars probe developed by the United Arab Emirates, and a week before NASA’s Perseverance Mars rover is scheduled for blastoff.
 The ever-changing positions of the planets only allow for missions to make a direct trip from Earth to Mars once every 26 months or so. The Mars launch window opened this year in mid-July and extends until mid-August.
 While NASA and U.S. scientists are aiding the UAE’s Hope Mars orbiter, NASA has no such role on China’s Tianwen 1 mission. NASA’s Deep Space Network, which provides tracking and communications coverage for numerous U.S. and international space probes, has not been called up to support Tianwen 1’s voyage to Mars.
 Instead, China is using a combination of its own tracking antennas and ESA’s global network of ground stations.
  “With today’s launch, China is on its way to join the community of international scientific explorers at Mars,” Bridenstine tweeted. “The United States, Europe, Russia, India, and soon the UAE will welcome you to Mars to embark on an exciting year of scientific discovery. Safe travels Tianwen-1!”

今天，Dealmaster的头条新闻是Bose的降噪耳机700有很好的折扣，在亚马逊上降到299美元。这笔交易只适用于白色的肥皂石型号，但这笔交易与我们迄今看到的无线降噪耳机的最低价格持平。这一折扣比Bose&39；的建议零售价低100美元，比该设备在网上的典型市场价格低约80美元。
虽然我们还没有正式审查网站上的降噪耳机700，但在过去的一年里，我们已经测试了它们的各种礼物指南。一般说来，对于那些在市场上购买高级无线降噪耳机的人来说，它们是一个强有力的选择。与索尼(Sony)的WH-1000XM3或百色(Bose)自己的QuietComfort 35 II等竞争对手相比，它们的金属设计有一种更高端的感觉，因此虽然它们的头部略重一些，但耳杯和头带上的充裕衬垫让它们更长时间佩戴起来很舒服。就音质而言，它们比更中性的QuietComfort 35 II更有低音前进性，虽然高音细节略低，但对于那些喜欢重低音的人来说，听起来仍然足够有趣。它们的平均电池续航时间也在20小时左右。
作为典型的Bose耳机，这里的有源消噪质量非常好。虽然在原始测量方面，它们的威力略逊于WH-1000XM3，但它们仍然能够静音环境声音和低端无人机。与索尼的耳机不同，消噪耳机700可以让你在10分的范围内自定义其消噪效果的强度，因此，如果你只想部分降低外部噪音，你可以做到。
不过，有几个警告值得注意。首先，如果你不需要更优质的设计和可定制的噪音消除，我们仍然认为WH-1000XM3是一款更好的整体配对：它们更轻，声音更全面，更可定制，它们的设计可以折叠，而且它们为触控提供了更大的空间。同样值得记住的是，索尼预计将在未来几周推出这些耳机的后续产品，可能会带来更好的声音、通话质量和多设备连接。
除此之外，降噪耳机700比通常更依赖配套应用程序进行功能控制。一些用户过去曾报告该应用程序的更新存在问题，以至于Bose最近完全取消了固件更新。这些问题似乎不会影响大多数用户，我们仍然认为这些耳机总体上是值得称赞的，但如果你喜欢更直截了当的耳机，你可能会想看看其他地方。

Today's Dealmaster is headlined by a nice discount on  Bose's Noise Cancelling Headphones 700, which are down to $299 on Amazon. The deal only applies to the white "Soapstone" model, but the deal matches the lowest price we've seen for the wireless noise-cancelling headphones to date. This discount is $100 off Bose's MSRP and about $80 off the device's typical street price online.
 While we haven't formally reviewed the Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 on the site, we have tested them for various gift guides over the past year. Generally speaking, they're a strong option for those in the market for a premium pair of wireless noise-cancelling headphones. Their metal design has a higher-end feel than competing pairs like  Sony's WH-1000XM3 or  Bose's own QuietComfort 35 II, and while they're slightly heavier on the head as a result, the ample padding on their ear cups and headband keeps them comfortable to wear for longer periods. In terms of audio quality, they have a more bass-forward sound than the more neutral QuietComfort 35 II, which results in a bit less treble detail but still sounds sufficiently "fun" for those who like punchy bass. They also average around 20 hours of battery life.
 As is typical of Bose headphones, the quality of the active noise cancellation here is excellent. Though they're  slightly less powerful than the WH-1000XM3 in raw measurements, they're still more than capable of muting ambient sounds and low-end droning. And unlike Sony's pair, the Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 give you the ability to customize the strength of their noise cancelling effect on a 10-point scale, so if you only want to partially tone down outside noise, you can.
 There are a few caveats worth noting, though. The first is that  we still find the WH-1000XM3 to be a better overall pair if you don't need the more premium design and customizable noise cancelling: they're lighter, their sound is slightly more well-rounded and customizable, their design folds up, and they give more room for their touch controls. It's also worth keeping in mind that Sony is expected to launch the follow-up to those headphones in the coming weeks,  potentially bringing improved sound, call quality, and multi-device connections with it.
 Beyond that, the Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 are more reliant than usual on a companion app for feature control. Some users have  reported issues with updates to that app in the past, to the point where  Bose recently pulled a firmware update completely. These issues don't appear to affect the majority of users, and we still think these are commendable headphones on the whole, but if you'd prefer a more straightforward pair, you might want to look elsewhere.
 If you don't need a new pair of noise-cancelling cans, though, we also have deals on Amazon devices, Samsung's Galaxy S10e, Fitbit activity trackers, and more. Have a look at the full rundown below.

随着SIZE视频预告片不太热闹的E3季接近尾声，微软周四为即将推出的Xbox Series X游戏机揭开了其最大规模的第一方力量秀。微软确认计划发布“光环无限”战役游戏后，这一系列游戏就开始大肆炒作。不过，这个故事也是关于哪些新的、熟悉的游戏系列与“酋长大师”同台亮相(以及没有出现)。
这场活动没有浪费时间，一切都是光环，还有人气人物科塔娜的画外音，讲述了首席盔甲大师的重组：液晶不能自己升起。不久之后，我们看到首席执行官乘坐宇宙飞船，由去年发现首席盔甲大师电影预告片的人驾驶，他们在讨论下一步该做什么。(注：原文为“首席盔甲大师”，原文为“首席盔甲大师”。)。这名男子说，如果不是因为我，你现在还会在外面。我以为我要回家了。
酋长回答说：如果我们不阻止被放逐的人，就不会有家。这一细节是第一次潜在的确认昨晚泄露的4chan确实是准确的，第二个迹象出现在不久之后，当泄密切换到现场游戏时-可以看到酋长挥舞着一个格斗钩子，他用这个钩子从远处钩住一个敌人，直接拉到它的脸上，然后用拳头打它。我们还第一次看到了带有内置地图的光环游戏，并标明了任务地点-证明了多年来的传言，即光环无限很可能是光环诗篇中的一场开放世界的冒险。
但除此之外，运行速度明显为60fps的演示序列，可能是你从主线Halo游戏中希望得到的一切：狡猾的AI可以分而治之，以及各种强大的武器来击败他们(包括至少一支新的UNSC步枪和一支新的外星人品牌激光发射器)。演示以2020；Holiday 2020的发布窗口结束，但目前还没有更具体的内容。(我们已经收到警告，此活动不会包括Xbox系列X的发布日期信息。)。
Xbox首席执行官菲尔·斯宾塞(Phil Spencer)证实，“光环无限”和活动期间播放的每一款其他游戏都将作为付费Xbox游戏通行证订阅服务的一部分首次亮相。
Forza MotorSports：这款车没有发布窗口，斯宾塞将其描述为正在开发中，这表明这款广受欢迎的Xbox独家赛车系列今年不会与Xbox Series X一起发布。取而代之的是，我们看到的是斯宾塞坚称完全由引擎内镜头制成的嘶嘶声卷轴，与Forza Horizon的开放式驾驶不同，它在赛车场更坚固的赛道上有着丰富的实时反光效果和多边形丰富的赛车。这与Forza Horizon的露天驾驶形成了鲜明的对比，斯宾塞坚称这一卷轴完全是由引擎内的镜头制成的，而且它具有丰富的实时反光效果和多边形丰富的赛车，与Forza Horizon的开放式驾驶截然不同。还有，难道你不知道吗，这款游戏机似乎不会在Xbox One的基础游戏机上推出！
腐烂状态3：这个非游戏性的镜头中，一个女人在雪地里猎鹿，结果发现这只鹿感染了某种僵尸般的杂草。
《荒野：罕见》这款之前曾被戏弄过的游戏收到了一场电影般的轰鸣声，卡通、奇幻英雄角色做着明显神秘的事情，比如举行降神会，让一只像鹿一样的生物起死回生。
告诉我原因：“奇异人生”(Life Is Strange)的团队唐诺娱乐(Dontnod Entertainment)透露了一个新的叙事驱动故事，故事发生在一个林地小镇，讲述了一对兄弟姐妹首次在Xbox游戏机和Windows 10上发布的故事。第一章将于8月27日发布，显然是为当前一代的机器设计的。
ORI和WIPS的意志：我们得到确认，这款之前发布的游戏将在Xbox Series X上跳跃到120fps的刷新率。
公开：黑曜石娱乐在透露了之前宣布的票价后，调侃了它的第一个Xbox独家RPG，这个简短的镜头隐约倾向于明显的天际般的灵感。
微软上一次尝试Xbox Series X游戏展示是在5月份，但结果有点平淡，因为它主要面向独立开发者；它为数不多的主要第三方参赛作品大多是预先渲染的镜头和不清晰的游戏。索尼热衷于利用这一点，在6月份推出了令人惊叹的PlayStation5独家产品和揭秘。
note_en:
As the not-quite-E3 season of sizzle video trailers comes to a close, Microsoft unveiled its biggest show of first-party force for its upcoming Xbox Series X console on Thursday. Hype began building for this one as soon as Microsoft confirmed plans to reveal  Halo Infinite campaign gameplay, but the story was just as much about which new and familiar game series did (and didn't) show up alongside Master Chief.
 The event wasted no time with all things  Halo, complete with a voiceover from the popular character Cortana narrating the recontsructing of the Master Chief armor set: "Liquid crystal cannot rise on its own." Shortly after, we see Chief in a spaceship, piloted by the man who discovered Chief in last year's cinematic trailer, as they argue about what to do next. "You'd still be out there if it wasn't for me," the man says. "I thought I was going home."
 "There won't be a home if we don't stop the Banished," Chief replies. This detail is the first potential confirmation that a 4chan leak from last night was actually accurate, and second indication came shortly after, once the reveal switched to live gameplay—and Chief is seen brandishing a grappling hook, which he uses to hook to a foe from a distance, zip directly into its face, and smack it with his fist. We also get a first glimpse ever at a  Halo game with a built-in map, complete with marked mission locations—proving out years of rumors that  Halo Infinite is likely an open-world adventure in the Halo-verse.
 But otherwise, the demo sequence, which ran at an apparent 60fps, is everything you might hope for from a mainline  Halo game: crafty AI that divides and conquers, and an assortment of powerful weapons to beat them with (including at least one new UNSC rifle and one new alien-branded laser launcher). The demo concluded with the release window of "Holiday 2020," but nothing more specific than that just yet. (We were already warned that this event would  not include release date info for Xbox Series X.)
  Xbox chief Phil Spencer confirmed that  Halo Infinite, and every other game shown during the event, will debut as part of the paid Xbox Game Pass subscription service.
     Forza Motorsport: No release window for this one, and Spencer described this as a "work-in-progress," suggesting that this popular Xbox-exclusive racing series won't launch alongside Xbox Series X this year. Instead, we were treated to a sizzle reel that Spencer insisted was made entirely of in-engine footage, and it was rich with glossy real-time reflection effects and polygon-rich racing cars on the firmer racetracks of the "Motorsport" fork, as opposed to the open-nature driving of  Forza Horizon. And, wouldn't you know it, this one does not appear to be slated to launch on base Xbox One consoles!
 State of Decay 3: This non-gameplay sequence saw a woman hunting a deer in a snowy landscape, only to discover the deer in question is infected with some sort of zombie-like overgrowth.
  Everwild: Rare's previously teased game received a cinematic sizzle reveal of cartoony, fantasy-hero characters doing apparently mystical stuff, like conducting a seance to bring a deer-like creature back to life.
 Tell Me Why: Dontnod Entertainment, the team behind  Life Is Strange, revealed a new narrative-driven story set in a small woodland town about a brother and a sister launching first on Xbox consoles and Windows 10. Its first chapter will arrive August 27, and it's clearly designed for current-gen machines.
 Ori and the Will of the Wisps: We got confirmation that this previously released game will jump to 120fps refresh rates on Xbox Series X.
  Avowed: Obsidian Entertainment teased its first Xbox-exclusive RPG after revealing previously announced fare, and the brief footage vaguely leans into obvious  Skyrim-like inspirations.
 Microsoft's last attempt at an Xbox Series X gameplay reveal in May fell a bit flat, since it focused primarily on indie developers; its few "major" third party entries consisted mostly of pre-rendered footage and unclear gameplay. Sony was keen to capitalize on this with  a stunning June presentation of PlayStation 5 exclusives and reveals.

领导对Facebook、苹果、亚马逊和谷歌进行全面反垄断调查的三名议员也持有其中一家或多家公司的股票，随着调查周一接近关键的作证日，这造成了潜在的利益冲突。
亚马逊首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯、苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克、Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格，以及拥有谷歌和YouTube的Alphabet的桑德尔·皮查伊将于周一在众议院司法委员会作证。议员们对科技公司是否滥用其市场主导地位以不公平地扼杀竞争进行了长达数月的调查，目前已接近尾声。
但根据众议员吉姆·森森布伦纳(Jim Sensenbrenner)-众议院反垄断小组委员会的资深成员-以及众议员佐伊·洛夫格伦(Zoe Lofgren)和史蒂夫·查伯特(Steve Chabot)最新披露的财务信息，他们也持有这些公司的股票。众议院司法委员会的所有三名成员将于周一受命盘问科技公司的首席执行官。
立法者持有公司的股份并不违法，即使在对这些公司进行调查的时候也是如此。但这些持股可能会造成潜在的利益冲突，或者破坏公众对调查的信任。
威斯康星州共和党人、反垄断委员会共和党最高代表森森布伦纳(Sensenbrenner)在这四家公司总共拥有超过9.8万美元的股票。根据他最新的财务披露，他拥有苹果26,658美元，亚马逊27,035美元，Alphabet 37,384美元和Facebook 7,341美元。
加州民主党人洛夫格伦(Lofgren)最近披露的财务信息显示，她分别持有Facebook、苹果和Alphabet价值1000至1.5万美元的股票。她报告说，她在过去一年里出售了每家公司的部分股票，但没有具体说明出售的具体金额。
查博特是来自俄亥俄州的共和党人，根据他最近的披露，他在Facebook拥有1.5万美元至5万美元的股票。
森森布伦纳的一位发言人表示，这位国会议员的股票由森森布伦纳已故父亲设立的一个信托基金持有，他并不积极管理投资组合。洛夫格伦的一位发言人说，她的股票是由她丈夫管理的展期爱尔兰共和军，她不管理所持股份。
这些人并不是第一批因持有他们负责调查的公司的股票而受到审查的立法者。统计新闻周一报道，众议员乔·肯尼迪(Joe Kennedy)和迈克尔·伯吉斯(Michael Burgess)是众议院一个委员会的成员，该委员会将就新冠肺炎的研究向制药公司提问，尽管两名众议员都拥有这些公司的股份。
司法委员会的调查旨在调查这四家科技巨头是否积累了太多的权力，以至于规模较小的竞争对手无法站稳脚跟。这项调查影响深远，可能成为针对科技公司权力的更广泛反弹的关键时刻。
周一的证词也备受期待，因为司法部正在准备可能对谷歌提起的反垄断诉讼，亚马逊和Facebook都是联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)正在进行的调查的对象。

note_en:
Three of the lawmakers leading a sweeping antitrust investigation into Facebook, Apple, Amazon, and Google also own stock in one or more of those companies, creating potential conflicts of interest as the investigation nears a pivotal day of testimony Monday.
 Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos, Apple CEO Tim Cook, Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg, and Sundar Pichai of Alphabet, which owns Google and YouTube, will testify Monday before the House Judiciary Committee. Lawmakers are approaching the end of a monthslong probe into whether the tech companies misused their market dominance to unfairly stifle competition.
 But Rep. Jim Sensenbrenner — who is the ranking member of the House Antitrust Subcommittee — and Reps. Zoe Lofgren and Steve Chabot also own stock in those companies, according to their latest financial disclosures. All three House Judiciary Committee Members will be tasked with grilling the tech CEOs on Monday.
 It's not illegal for lawmakers to own shares in companies, even when an investigation into those companies is underway. But the holdings  could create potential conflicts of interest or undermine public trust in the investigation.
   Sensenbrenner, a Wisconsin Republican and the top GOP representative on the antitrust committee, owns more than $98,000 of stock in the four companies combined. He owns $26,658 in Apple, $27,035 in Amazon, $37,384 in Alphabet, and $7,341 in Facebook, according to his  latest financial disclosure.
 Lofgren, a California Democrat, owns between $1,000 and $15,000 of stock in Facebook, Apple, and Alphabet each, according to her  most recent financial disclosure. She  reported that she sold some of those shares in each company in the past year, but did not specify the exact amount that was sold.
 Chabot, a Republican from Ohio, owns between $15,000 and $50,000 of stock in Facebook, according to  his most recent disclosure.
 A spokesperson for Sensenbrenner said the congressman's shares are in a trust set up by Sensenbrenner's late father and that he does not actively manage the portfolio. A spokesperson for Lofgren said her shares are in a rollover IRA managed by her husband and that she does not manage the holdings.
    These aren't the first lawmakers to draw scrutiny for owning shares of companies they're tasked with investigating. Stat News  reported Monday that Reps. Joe Kennedy and Michael Burgess are members of a House committee that will question pharma companies about COVID-19 research, despite the fact that both representatives own shares of those companies.
 The Judiciary Committee investigation is meant to dig into whether the four tech giants have accumulated so much power that it's become impossible for smaller competitors to gain a foothold. The investigation is far-reaching and could be a pivotal moment in broader backlash against tech companies' power.
 Monday's testimony is also hotly anticipated as the Justice Department is preparing a  possible antitrust suit against Google, and both Amazon and Facebook are the subject of an ongoing  Federal Trade Commission investigation.

title: 3名负责就反垄断问题盘问苹果、亚马逊、谷歌和Facebook的议员持有这些公司数千股股票
title_en: 3 lawmakers in charge of grilling Apple, Amazon, Google, and Facebook on antitrust own thousands in stock in those companies
